back: A software component is a modular, deployable, and replaceable part of a system that encapsulates its implementation and provides a set of interfaces. It is a self-contained unit of functionality that can be composed with other components to build larger applications.

front: What are the key characteristics of a well-designed component?
back: Key characteristics include: high cohesion (all parts are strongly related), low coupling (minimal dependencies on other components), encapsulation (hides internal implementation), reusability (can be used in multiple contexts), and replaceability (can be swapped with another component providing the same interface).

front: What is the difference between a component and a module?
back: A module is a code-based construct (like a class or package) used for structuring code within a development environment. A component is a runtime entity that is independently deployable and has explicit context dependencies. All components are modules, but not all modules are components.

Component-Based Development is a software engineering paradigm that emphasizes the design and construction of computer-based systems using reusable software components. It focuses on assembling systems from pre-existing components rather than building them from scratch, aiming to reduce cost and time-to-market.

A component model defines standards and guidelines that components must follow to be reusable and interoperable. It specifies how components are implemented, packaged, deployed, and communicate. Examples include the CORBA Component Model (CCM), Enterprise JavaBeans (EJB), and the .NET component model.
